channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Filmmakers Zig Dulay and Ruel Santos Bayani Collaborate on Upcoming Projects with CreaZion Studios
item.description: Filmmakers Zig Dulay and Ruel Santos Bayani are joining forces to create new cinematic projects under the banner of CreaZion Studios. Both directors are known in the Philippine film industry for produ...

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak on Atlantic expedition ship challenges administration's public health stance
item.description: A hantavirus outbreak on an expedition ship in the Atlantic Ocean has resulted in the deaths of three passengers, raising concerns about the handling of emerging infectious threats. This incident pres...

item.title: Historical Headache Treatments from Trepanation to Vibration Therapy
item.description: Headaches have plagued humans for thousands of years, leading to a variety of often extreme treatments. In the 1890s, a method called vibration therapy involved placing a patient's head in a helmet an...

item.title: Guided Tour Offers Public a First Look Inside Toronto’s CN Tower
item.description: As part of its 50th anniversary celebrations, Toronto's CN Tower is opening sections normally closed to the public. Later this month, the iconic 553-metre tower will participate in Doors Open Toronto,...

item.title: Mirerani Holds Transparent Online Auction for Tanzanite Gemstones with 100 Licensed Brokers
item.description: In Mirerani, a small mining town in Simanjiro District, Manyara Region, 100 licensed small-scale gemstone traders participated in a Tanzanite gemstone auction, conducted at the Mirerani gemstone marke...

item.title: Our Lady’s College to accept boys from 2030 as two nearby primary schools close
item.description: Brisbane Catholic Education (BCE) has announced major changes affecting several schools in south-east Queensland. Our Lady’s College in Annerley, currently a Catholic girls’ high school, will become c...

item.title: Balancing Domestic and Foreign-Based Players in Nigeria’s National Football Team
item.description: Dis article dey talk say Nigerian football don get one wahala wey concern how we dey select players for Super Eagles. E highlight say many of di players wey dey do well abroad start dem career for Nai...

item.title: National Assembly Defends Gachagua Impeachment as Fair and Constitutional
item.description: Lawyers for the National Assembly have defended the impeachment of former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua, rejecting claims that he was denied a fair chance to defend himself. During the hearing of ...

item.title: Civil Society Urges Extension for Public Input on Whistleblower Protection Bill
item.description: Civil society organisations, led by Corruption Watch, are urging Justice Minister Mmamoloko Kubayi to extend the deadline for public submissions on the proposed Protected Disclosures Bill, 2026. The B...

item.title: Chernobyl Workers Prevented a Nuclear Explosion in 1986
item.description: Ten days after the catastrophic Chernobyl nuclear disaster in 1986, a new and potentially more devastating threat emerged. A large pool of water had accumulated beneath Reactor 4's molten core, and ex...

item.title: Actor Donald Gibb, Known for ‘Revenge of the Nerds’ and ‘Bloodsport,’ Passes Away at 71
item.description: Donald Gibb, the American actor celebrated for his roles in cult classics such as 'Revenge of the Nerds' and 'Bloodsport,' has died at the age of 71. According to TMZ, Gibb's son, Travis, confirmed th...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of “The Young Girls of Rochefort” (1967)
item.description: “The Young Girls of Rochefort” (Les Demoiselles de Rochefort), directed by Jacques Demy in 1967, is a French musical comedy celebrated for its vibrant pastel colors, jazzy soundtrack, and homage to cl...

item.title: Federal Court Rules Coles Misled Shoppers with 'Down Down' Promotions
item.description: The Federal Court has found Coles guilty of misleading consumers over its 'Down Down' promotions, in a case brought by the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC). Justice Michael O'Brya...

item.title: Tanzania Lutheran Church to adopt single constitution for stronger leadership
item.description: Di Evangelical Lutheran Church in Tanzania (ELCT) iko preparing for big changes as dem plan to bring one General Assembly before end of dis year to approve new constitution. Dis move follows decision ...

item.title: A Look at American Life in the Late 1940s Through Color Photography
item.description: Life in the United States during the late 1940s was marked by a mix of relief, optimism, and transformation as the country recovered from World War II. This period, known as the Post-War Boom, saw sig...

item.title: NBI Agents Suggest Senate Disturbance May Have Been Orchestrated
item.description: The recent commotion in the Philippine Senate has sparked debate over whether the incident was genuinely chaotic or deliberately staged. According to National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Secretary M...

item.title: UK economy shows growth despite global tensions over Iran conflict
item.description: New data on the United Kingdom's economic performance has revealed that the economy experienced growth at a time of international tension surrounding the outbreak of conflict involving Iran. Despite e...

item.title: Nigeria and Rwanda to Strengthen Cooperation in Trade, Tourism, and Anti-Corruption
item.description: On Wednesday, President Bola Tinubu of Nigeria and President Paul Kagame of Rwanda meet for proper talks for Kigali during the Africa CEO Forum. Dem yan say make dem reactivate the Joint Permanent Min...

item.title: How Prospect Theory Influences Investment Decisions and Risk Management
item.description: Fisher Investments Canada discusses the relevance of prospect theory, first explored by psychologists Daniel Kahneman and Amos Tversky nearly 50 years ago, to modern investing. The theory explains hum...

item.title: ConCourt to Consider Legality of Magudumana's Extradition from Tanzania
item.description: The Constitutional Court of South Africa is set to hear Nandipha Magudumana's challenge regarding the legality of her extradition from Tanzania back to South Africa in April 2023. Magudumana was arres...
